g.text: A Comprehensive Visual Guide to Bottom-Up LR Parsing

g.data-narration: Bottom-up parsing builds parse trees from leaves to root. LR parsers scan input left-to-right, producing rightmost derivations in reverse.
g.text: • Builds parse tree from leaves to root
Most powerful class of bottom-up parsers

g.data-narration: SLR is a practical compromise - more powerful than LR(0) but easier than full LR(1), using FOLLOW sets for lookahead decisions.
g.text: LR Parsing Family - Power vs Complexity

g.data-narration: An LR(0) item is a production with a dot showing recognition progress. The dot position tells us how much has been parsed so far.
g.text: LR(0) Items: Productions with a Dot (•)
For production A → XYZ, the possible items are:

g.data-narration: Closure expands item sets by adding productions for non-terminals after the dot. This predicts what might come next in parsing.
g.text: If closure contains [A → α•Bβ], add [B → •γ] for all B → γ

g.data-narration: The Goto operation moves the dot across a symbol, then computes closure. This defines transitions between parser states.
g.text: Move dot past X in items of I, then take closure

g.data-narration: The Canonical Collection builds all parser states by repeatedly computing goto on all symbols until no new states emerge.
g.text: Building Canonical Collection of LR(0) Items
Algorithm: Start with I₀ = closure({[S' → •S]}), then compute goto for all symbols

g.data-narration: The ACTION table tells shift/reduce/accept decisions. SLR uses FOLLOW sets for reduce actions - this is what makes it 'Simple' LR.
g.text: • Shift: [A→α•aβ] in Iᵢ, goto(Iᵢ,a)=Iⱼ → ACTION[i,a]="shift j"
• Reduce: [A→α•] in Iᵢ, a∈FOLLOW(A) → ACTION[i,a]="reduce A→α"
• goto(Iᵢ, A) = Iⱼ → GOTO[i, A] = j
🔑 SLR's Key: Uses FOLLOW(A) for reduce actions, not just any lookahead

g.data-narration: The parser uses a stack of states and symbols. On shift, push symbol and new state. On reduce, pop, push non-terminal, push goto state.
g.text: if ACTION[s,a] = shift t: push a, push t
if ACTION[s,a] = reduce A→β: pop 2|β|, push GOTO

g.data-narration: Let's build an SLR table for this grammar with start symbol E. We need augmented grammar, first sets, and follow sets.
Here's the complete SLR parsing table. States I2 and I3 contain reduce items, so we use FOLLOW(E) and FOLLOW(T) for lookahead.
g.text: SLR Parsing Table for E → E+T | T | id
r1: E→E+T, r2: E→T, r3: T→id

g.data-narration: SLR balances power and practicality. It resolves more conflicts than LR(0) but needs smaller tables than canonical LR(1).
